identity.name: Cloud App — Google Full-Mailbox + Offline Token Replay Risk (T1114.002)
identity.summary: An OAuth authorize request to the Google Identity endpoint (accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth) requests https://mail.google.com/ full-mailbox scope, and a parallel Microsoft grant requests Mail.ReadWrite + offline_access. Cortex Cloud App Security flags full-mailbox scopes, treats offline_access (refresh token) as a token-replay risk, and an analytics layer stitches the same identity targeti…
identity.description: Granting a third-party app the full Gmail scope (https://mail.google.com/) or the Microsoft Mail.ReadWrite scope together with offline_access hands an attacker durable, MFA-independent remote access to a victim's mailbox — the long-lived refresh token can be replayed for months to read, exfiltrate, and send mail. This is the precise grant shape seen in commodity OAuth mailbox-hijack phishing campaigns. This scenario drives oauth_grant_emulator with the full_mailbox preset against Google (requesting https://mail.google.com/ and gmail.modify) and then against Microsoft (requesting Mail.ReadWrite, Mail.Send, offline_access). Cortex Cloud App Security fires on the full-mailbox scope on the google_workspace_audit event and, separately, on the Mail.ReadWrite + offline_access combination on the msft_o365_audit event — the offline_access scope being the token-replay primitive. A cross-provider analytics rule recognises the same identity soliciting mailbox access at two providers inside the rotation window, and a XSIAM stitching rule fuses both grants into one mailbox-collection incident. All requests use bogus client_ids against canary endpoints, so no token is ever issued — detection is on the requested-scope shape, not a granted token.

techniques.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token

prerequisites.description: oauth_grant_emulator supports the google provider and Cortex Cloud App Security ingests Google Workspace + Microsoft 365 OAuth grant audit events.
Outbound HTTPS egress to accounts.google.com and login.microsoftonline.com passes through the in-path NGFW EAL stack.

payload.code:
# Full Gmail mailbox scope grant against the canary Google authorize endpoint — bogus client_id, no token issued.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/cloud-003.yml --live
# Parallel Microsoft Mail.ReadWrite + offline_access grant — same identity, second mail provider.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/cloud-003-ms.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Google OAuth grant audit event requesting the full mail.google.com scope.
Microsoft OAuth grant audit event requesting Mail.ReadWrite together with offline_access.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: dataset = msft_o365_audit and oauth_scopes contains "Mail.ReadWrite" and grant_type = "offline"

biocs.name: CLOUD-003 Pre-Flight oauth_grant_emulator Plugin Registered
biocs.description: Pre-flight: oauth_grant_emulator metadata visible to operator. Confirms the EAL plugin is registered and its eal_targets enumerate the Cloud App OAuth-grant detections before any grant is emitted.
biocs.logic:
dataset = saas_audit_logs
| filter event_type = "eal_plugin_describe" or action contains "oauth_grant_emulator"
| filter app_name = "oauth_grant_emulator"
| fields _time, user, app_name, action

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-003 Google Full Mailbox Scope Grant Attempt
xql_queries.query:
dataset = google_workspace_audit
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter oauth_scopes contains_any ("mail.google.com", "gmail.modify", "gmail.readonly")
| filter client_id != null
| fields _time, user, app_name, client_id, oauth_scopes, consent_type, source_ip, geo

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-003 NGFW Google OAuth Authorize Mailbox-Scope Egress
xql_queries.query:
dataset = saas_audit_logs
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "accounts.google.com"
| filter uri_path contains "/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
| filter oauth_scopes contains "mail.google.com"
| fields _time, user, dst_host, uri_path, oauth_scopes, source_ip

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-003 Microsoft Mail ReadWrite Offline Access Token-Replay Grant
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_o365_audit
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter oauth_scopes contains "Mail.ReadWrite"
| filter oauth_scopes contains "offline_access" or grant_type in ("offline", "refresh")
| fields _time, user, app_name, client_id, oauth_scopes, grant_type, source_ip

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-003 Cross-Provider Mailbox Collection Same Identity Stitch
xql_queries.query:
dataset = saas_audit_logs
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter oauth_scopes contains_any ("mail.google.com", "gmail.modify", "Mail.ReadWrite", "Mail.Send")
| comp count_distinct(app_name) as mail_providers, values(app_name) as providers by user
| filter mail_providers >= 2
| fields user, mail_providers, providers

correlation_rules.name: Cross-Provider OAuth Mailbox Takeover Token Replay — Same Identity
correlation_rules.logic: XQL(CLOUD-003 Google Full Mailbox Scope Grant Attempt) OR XQL(CLOUD-003 Microsoft Mail ReadWrite Offline Access Token-Replay Grant) OR XQL(CLOUD-003 Cross-Provider Mailbox Collection Same Identity Stitch)

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud App Security — Third-party app requests full-mailbox scope
Cortex Cloud App Security — offline_access refresh-token grant flagged as token-replay risk
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Same identity solicits mailbox access at two providers

products.evidence_query: dataset = google_workspace_audit | filter oauth_scopes contains "mail.google.com" | fields _time, user, app_name, oauth_scopes
Correlation CR-CLOUDAPP-0003 fuses the Google full-mailbox grant, the Microsoft Mail.ReadWrite+offline_access grant, and the cross-provider same-identity stitch into one mailbox-collection incident.

use_cases.name: Mailbox Takeover via OAuth Detection
use_cases.description: Detect full-mailbox and offline-access OAuth grants and stitch a single identity targeting two mail providers into one mailbox-collection incident.

test_cases.objective: Full-mailbox and offline-access grants fire their Cloud App detections.
preconditions: oauth_grant_emulator google provider available and Workspace + M365 audit logs ingested.
Detections from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Observe the Google full-mailbox alert and the Microsoft offline-access alert.
success_criteria: XQL `CLOUD-003 Google Full Mailbox Scope Grant Attempt` returns >= 1 row.
XQL `CLOUD-003 Microsoft Mail ReadWrite Offline Access Token-Replay Grant` returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Same identity at two mail providers stitches into one incident.
success_criteria: XQL `CLOUD-003 Cross-Provider Mailbox Collection Same Identity Stitch` returns >= 1 row.

preventive_controls: Block third-party apps requesting full-mailbox scopes via app-governance policy.
Treat offline_access / refresh-token grants to unverified publishers as high risk and require admin review.
Revoke and re-issue tokens on any suspected mailbox-grant; enforce short token lifetimes.
detection_engineering: Alert on https://mail.google.com/, gmail.modify, and Mail.ReadWrite scope grants to third-party apps.
Flag offline_access / refresh-token grants as token-replay risk distinct from short-lived access tokens.
Correlate the same identity granting mailbox scopes at multiple providers within a short window.

references.title: Mandiant - OAuth Mailbox Hijack via Phishing Apps
MITRE ATT&CK T1114.002 — Remote Email Collection
